name: Sky Harbour Group Corp
business: Sky Harbour Group Corp is an aviation infrastructure development company that develops, leases, and manages general aviation hangars designed exclusively for business aircraft. The company targets high-end tenants in markets with hangar supply shortages. Its revenues are primarily derived from long-term rental agreements.

exposures.sentence: We are an aviation infrastructure development company building the first nationwide network of Home Base Operator (HBO) campuses designed exclusively for business aircraft.
We operate a fleet of electric ground support equipment which have a low cost to operate and maintain.
our hangars are designed to be both solar and wind energy capable for future installation.

catalysts.sentence: ADS Phase II In Construction Q2 2025 Q1 2027 - Estimated Total Construction Cost $24.6 - $27.6 million, Hangars 4, Rentable Square Footage 110,990
BDL Phase I In Construction Q3 2025 Q4 2026 - Estimated Total Construction Cost $40.0 - $42.1 million, Hangars 3, Rentable Square Footage 107,360
OPF Phase II In Construction Q1 2025 Q2 2026 - Estimated Total Construction Cost $39.3 - $39.6 million, Hangars 3, Rentable Square Footage 111,201

tamClaims.claim: The cumulative square footage of the business aircraft fleet in the United States increased 73% between 2010 and 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: The cumulative square footage of the business aircraft fleet in the United States increased 73% between 2010 and 2025.

tamClaims.claim: up to 8,500 new business jet deliveries worth over $283 billion expected to be delivered between 2025 and 2034
tamClaims.sentence: A recent study conducted by a business aircraft manufacturer forecasted that business aircraft will only continue to grow in the next ten years, with up to 8,500 new business jet deliveries worth over $283 billion expected to be delivered between 2025 and 2034, with over two-thirds of the deliveries expected to be comprised of larger private jets.

tamClaims.claim: current order backlog for new business aviation aircraft as of December 31, 2025 is over $57 billion
tamClaims.sentence: This forecast is further supported by data from the major business aviation manufacturers that suggest the current order backlog for new business aviation aircraft as of December 31, 2025 is over $57 billion, an increase of approximately 10% over the prior year.

tamClaims.claim: The physical footprint of the U.S. business aviation fleet grew by almost 46 million square feet in the past sixteen years
tamClaims.sentence: The physical footprint of the U.S. business aviation fleet grew by almost 46 million square feet in the past sixteen years, with hangar supply lagging dramatically, especially in key growth markets.
